articles.title: HUMANS SUCK AT PROJECT MANAGEMENT
articles.summary: We’ve all tried every project management tool under the sun… And we’ve all given up on most of them at one point or another.

articles.title: PROJECT GUTENBERG RELEASES THOUSANDS OF AI AUDIOBOOKS
articles.summary: In collaboration with Microsoft, thousands of open-domain books have been transcribed with state-of-the-art text-to-speech. They're available on most streaming platforms. Information about the process is included in the link.

articles.title: META IS WORKING ON A GPT-4 COMPETITOR
articles.summary: Meta has been snapping up AI training chips and building out data centers in order to create a more powerful new chatbot it hopes will be as sophisticated as OpenAI’s GPT-4.

articles.title: SPEED UP SPECULATIVE DECODING WITHOUT A DRAFT MODEL
articles.summary: Language model inference is generally slow because these models are heavily memory-bound. So people have introduced speculative decoding using a smaller draft model to “run ahead” and offer suggestions to the large model. This works well, but it’s complicated to implement, and finding a good draft model is tough. Instead, you can create a Medusa model that acts as its own draft by splitting off activations from various internal stages. This speeds up Llama 2x without compromising performance.

articles.title: A BETTER UNDERSTANDING AND REASONING ABOUT PICTURES
articles.summary: The authors of this study have created a way to test how well vision-language models can also think logically about what they see.

articles.title: MAKING SELF-DRIVING CARS SMARTER WITH LANGUAGE PROMPTS
articles.summary: This project introduces NuPrompt, a new set of instructions in everyday language that helps self-driving cars understand and track objects around them better. It works really well!

articles.title: RESTGPT
articles.summary: RestGPT aims to construct a large language model-based autonomous agent to control real-world applications, such as movie databases and music players.

articles.title: TEACHING LANGUAGE MODELS TO UNDERSTAND 3D SHAPES
articles.summary: PointLLM is a tool that helps language models understand 3D objects, not just text and 2D images.

articles.title: CAN LLMS REASON?
articles.summary: The debate surrounding large language models concerns whether they possess human-like reasoning abilities or rely on memorization and pattern-matching, with studies showing mixed results. Evaluating LLMs for true abstract reasoning is challenging due to their tendency to excel in tasks related to their training data, necessitating further research for clarification.

articles.title: PATTERNS FOR BUILDING LLM-BASED SYSTEMS & PRODUCTS
articles.summary: This article shares seven practical patterns for integrating large language models into systems: measuring performance, adding recent knowledge, fine-tuning for tasks, caching to save time/cost, ensuring quality output, managing errors, and harnessing user feedback.

articles.title: ANOTHER GROUP OF WRITERS IS SUING OPENAI OVER COPYRIGHT CLAIMS
articles.summary: OpenAI faces a lawsuit from writers alleging illegal use of their copyrighted works to train ChatGPT. They argue that ChatGPT's capabilities to summarize and analyze their content indicate direct training on their works, constituting copyright infringement. This follows similar suits from other authors against OpenAI.

articles.title: OPENAI CONFIRMS THAT AI WRITING DETECTORS DON’T WORK
articles.summary: OpenAI has confirmed that AI writing detectors are ineffective.
